skill.name: css-animations
skill.description: CSS animation adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when authoring CSS keyframes, animation-delay based timing, animation-fill-mode, animation-play-state, or CSS-only motion that HyperFrames must seek deterministically during preview and rendering.
skill.summary: Write CSS keyframe animations that seek deterministically when rendered as video by HyperFrames
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 26 potential issues across 1 file (125 lines), but all are false positives. Markdown backtick formatting was misidentified as shell command execution, YAML frontmatter was misidentified as a weak cryptographic algorithm, CSS code inside markdown blocks was misidentified as system reconnaissance, and MDN documentation URLs in a references section were flagged as network calls. The skill is purely instructional documentation with no executable code, network requests, file system access, or malicious intent.

content.user_title: Create Deterministic CSS Animations for Video Rendering
content.value_statement: CSS animations in video rendering break during seeking because they lack deterministic state management. This skill provides adapter patterns for writing CSS keyframe animations that seek reliably in HyperFrames previews and renders.
actual_capabilities: Guide CSS keyframe animation authoring for deterministic frame-seeking in video output
Explain animation-fill-mode and animation-delay patterns required by the HyperFrames runtime adapter
Provide stagger animation patterns using CSS custom properties to avoid duplicating keyframes
Identify when CSS animations are preferable to JavaScript timeline libraries like GSAP
Show correct data-attribute markup for timed elements so the adapter can seek playback
Recommend finite iteration counts and fill-mode settings for predictable seeking behavior
limitations: Cannot seek infinite CSS animations without WAAPI-backed browser animation handles
Does not generate animations automatically — provides patterns and guidance only
CSS animations are less suitable for multi-element scene choreography than GSAP timelines
Requires understanding of the HyperFrames runtime adapter contract and data attributes

use_cases.title: Adding decorative motion to video compositions
use_cases.description: Composition authors add shimmer, glow, mask, and pulse effects that render as deterministic video frames.

use_cases.title: Building reusable CSS animation templates
use_cases.description: Frontend developers create template compositions with CSS-driven entrances and loops that export correctly.

use_cases.title: Replacing complex JavaScript timelines with CSS
use_cases.description: Motion designers simplify single-element animations by using native CSS keyframes instead of full JS timelines.

prompt_templates.title: Add a fade-in entrance animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add a CSS fade-in animation to a div element that HyperFrames can seek correctly during preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner learning the basic CSS animation pattern for HyperFrames

prompt_templates.title: Create staggered dot animations
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CSS-only staggered dot loading animation using custom properties and animation-delay for HyperFrames.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user wanting to avoid duplicating keyframes for multiple elements

prompt_templates.title: Build a pulse ring effect
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I make a CSS pulse ring animation that seeks correctly across different time points and holds its final state?
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needing specific fill-mode and timing patterns

prompt_templates.title: Convert GSAP timeline to CSS animations
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a GSAP timeline for single-element decorative motion. Rewrite it using CSS-only animations that work with the HyperFrames adapter.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert user optimizing performance by replacing JavaScript timelines with native CSS

output_examples.input: Add a shimmer effect to a logo element for 3 seconds
output_examples.output: Set animation-name to a custom shimmer keyframe with animation-duration: 3000ms, animation-iteration-count: 1, and animation-fill-mode: both. Add data-start and data-duration attributes so the adapter can seek.

output_examples.input: Create entrance animations for three text items with staggered timing
output_examples.output: Use CSS custom properties with --i on each child span. Set animation-delay: calc(var(--i) * 120ms) so each item starts after the previous one without duplicating keyframes.

output_examples.input: Why does my CSS animation jump back to the start when I seek backwards?
output_examples.output: Your element is missing animation-fill-mode: both. Without it, the browser resets to the initial keyframe state when the animation is outside its active duration during a seek.

best_practices: Use finite animation-iteration-count so the negative-delay fallback can represent the full animation duration
Set animation-fill-mode: both so elements hold their visual state before and after the active animation period
Add data-start, data-duration, and data-track-index attributes to every timed element for the CSS adapter
anti_patterns: Do not use animation-iteration-count: infinite — it breaks seeking because the negative-delay fallback cannot represent unbounded duration
Avoid hover, scroll, focus, or media query triggers for render-critical motion that must render deterministically
Do not change animation classes after startup unless another deterministic timeline controls that change

faq.question: When should I use CSS animations instead of GSAP?
faq.answer: Use CSS for simple single-element loops, shimmer, glow, and masks. Use GSAP for complex multi-element choreography with sequenced timelines.

faq.question: Can I use infinite CSS animations in HyperFrames?
faq.answer: Avoid infinite animations unless the browser supports seekable WAAPI-backed CSS animation handles. Use a finite iteration count covering the visible duration instead.

faq.question: What data attributes does the CSS adapter need?
faq.answer: Timed elements need data-start for clip time, data-duration for the element's visible window, and data-track-index so the adapter can seek correctly.

faq.question: Why does my animation jump when I seek backwards?
faq.answer: Set animation-fill-mode: both so the element holds its computed style before and after the active animation period during a seek.

faq.question: How do I create staggered animations without duplicating keyframes?
faq.answer: Use CSS custom properties like --i on each child element and set animation-delay: calc(var(--i) * 120ms) to offset each animation start.

faq.question: How do I validate my CSS animation composition?
faq.answer: Run npx hyperframes lint to check for structural issues and npx hyperframes validate to verify the composition renders correctly.
